assertion_results.text: A base error class (AppError or similar) exists with properties: code, message, statusCode, category, and cause
assertion_results.evidence: No base error class. Routes use inline res.status().json() with varying object shapes.

assertion_results.text: At least 5 error subclasses are defined covering: validation (400), authentication (401), authorization (403), not-found (404), and conflict (409)
assertion_results.evidence: No error subclasses. A single HttpError class exists with only statusCode and message, no category or code.

assertion_results.text: Each error class has a stable string error code (e.g., ERR_NOT_FOUND_USER) not just an HTTP status number
assertion_results.evidence: No string error codes. HttpError uses numeric status codes only.

assertion_results.text: A centralized error middleware function handles all errors and produces a consistent JSON response format
assertion_results.evidence: A basic error middleware exists that catches HttpError and returns { error: err.message, status: err.statusCode }. However the format differs from inline error responses in routes.

assertion_results.text: Error responses include a requestId or correlationId field for debugging
assertion_results.evidence: No requestId or correlationId in any error response.

assertion_results.text: User-facing error responses do not contain stack traces, file paths, or internal implementation details
assertion_results.evidence: The catch-all in error middleware returns err.message for unknown errors, which can expose MongoDB driver errors and file paths.

assertion_results.text: Structured error logging includes at minimum: error code, message, stack trace, correlation ID, and request path
assertion_results.evidence: Error middleware uses console.error(err) which dumps the full error object. No structured format, no correlation ID.

assertion_results.text: The error response JSON schema is consistent across all endpoints with at least code and message fields
assertion_results.evidence: Inconsistent: middleware returns { error, status }, but POST route returns { message: 'Validation failed', errors: [...] } and GET /:id returns { error: 'Not found' }.
